01_initial_request.input_data: Kidney tissue from healthy C57BL/6 mice
01_initial_request.user_intent: To comprehensively characterize the mouse kidney matrisome composition and create a reference database for future renal research due to limited existing data
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Identification and label-free quantification (LFQ) of matrisome proteins

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Healthy mouse kidney tissue is required as the starting material for matrisome characterization. [Gap] Tissue has not yet been collected or prepared. [Decision] To achieve tissue acquisition, chose to use Animal Dissection Protocol to perform euthanasia and kidney extirpation on C57BL/6 mice, expecting to obtain fresh kidney halves for extraction.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Kidney tissues collected and kept on ice, transversely cut into halves for parallel processing.

references.citation: UNSW Animal Care and Ethics Committee (ACEC) protocol 21/24A
references.relevance: Ethical approval and protocol for animal tissue collection.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] ECM proteins need to be enriched by removing cellular components. [Gap] Standard lysis retains high abundance intracellular proteins. [Decision] To achieve ECM enrichment via one method, chose to use Millipore Compartment Protein Extraction Kit to perform Compartmental Matrix Enrichment (CME) on one kidney half, expecting to obtain an insoluble ECM-enriched pellet.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: ECM enriched pellets obtained after depletion of cytosolic, nuclear, membrane and cytoskeletal proteins.

references.relevance: Describes the CME method for ECM enrichment.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] CME may miss loosely bound or soluble matrisome proteins. [Gap] Comprehensive characterization requires alternative extraction. [Decision] To achieve broader ECM coverage, chose to use Sequential Matrix Enrichment (SME) Protocol to perform sequential buffer extractions on the other kidney half, expecting to obtain three fractions (F1, F2, F3) containing different ECM protein subsets.

parameters.fraction_1_buffer: 0.5 M NaCl, 10 mM Tris-HCl, 25 mM EDTA, pH 7.5
parameters.fraction_3_buffer: 4 M guanidine hydrochloride, 50 mM Na acetate, 25 mM EDTA, pH 5.8

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Three fractions saved: F1 (loosely bound), F2 (intracellular removal/SDS), F3 (insoluble ECM solubilized).

references.relevance: Describes the SME method for ECM protein isolation.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Extracted samples contain detergents and salts incompatible with MS. [Gap] Samples need cleaning and concentration. [Decision] To achieve sample cleanup, chose to use Ethanol Precipitation to perform protein precipitation on CME pellets and SME fractions, expecting to obtain dried protein pellets free of detergents.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Protein precipitates obtained and dried pellets resuspended in 8M urea buffer.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Glycosylation can hinder peptide identification in MS. [Gap] Glycans need to be removed for better sequence coverage. [Decision] To achieve deglycosylation, chose to use PNGaseF to perform enzymatic removal of N-glycans on resuspended pellets, expecting to obtain deglycosylated protein samples.

references.relevance: Protocol for deglycosylation in matrisome studies.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Proteins must be digested into peptides for LC-MS/MS analysis. [Gap] Intact proteins are not suitable for shotgun proteomics. [Decision] To achieve peptide generation, chose to use Trypsin/Lys-C Mix to perform in-solution digestion on deglycosylated samples, expecting to obtain peptide mixtures ready for MS.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Digestion reaction completed, acidified supernatants saved for desalting.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Peptides need to be separated and detected to identify protein sequences. [Gap] Peptide composition and abundance are unknown. [Decision] To achieve peptide analysis, chose to use QExactive Plus mass spectrometer to perform LC-MS/MS on desalted peptides, expecting to obtain raw MS files containing spectral data.

parameters.column: fritless nanocolumn (75 μm x 20 cm) C18AQ

02_agent_trajectory.observation: MS raw files acquired for downstream computational analysis.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Raw MS data needs to be converted into protein identities and quantities. [Gap] Spectra are not yet assigned to peptides or proteins. [Decision] To achieve protein identification and quantification, chose to use MaxQuant to perform database searching and LFQ calculation on raw files, expecting to obtain protein groups table with LFQ intensities.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Total of 2442 proteins identified; LFQ intensity values generated for quantification.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Total protein list includes non-ECM proteins. [Gap] Specific matrisome components need to be filtered and classified. [Decision] To achieve matrisome classification, chose to use Matrisome Database (Matrisome Annotator) to perform filtering of unique peptides against known matrisome categories, expecting to obtain a curated list of matrisome proteins.

parameters.criteria: unique peptides present in at least two of the biological replicates
parameters.categories: core matrisome (collagens, glycoproteins, proteoglycans) and matrisome-associated (affiliated, regulators, secreted)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 113 matrisome proteins identified, including 22 not previously listed in the MD.

references.relevance: Definition and database of the matrisome.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Functional roles of identified proteins need to be understood. [Gap] Biological processes and molecular functions are not annotated in the protein list. [Decision] To achieve functional annotation, chose to use UniProt Database to perform Gene Ontology (GO) enrichment analysis on identified proteins, expecting to obtain GO terms for biological processes and molecular functions.

parameters.search_terms: extracellular matrix, extracellular space, basement membrane, secreted, lysosome, exosome

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Top GO terms identified: cellular process, developmental process, binding, regulation of catalytic activity.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Quantitative differences between extraction methods need statistical validation. [Gap] LFQ intensities from MaxQuant require differential analysis. [Decision] To achieve statistical comparison, chose to use LFQ-Analyst to perform differential abundant analysis on LFQ intensity values, expecting to obtain fold-changes and p-values for protein abundance differences.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Significant differences identified between CME and SME fractions for specific matrisome proteins.

references.relevance: LFQ-Analyst platform for visualizing label-free proteomics data.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: LC-MS/MS proteomics with Label-Free Quantification (LFQ) and comparative analysis against public databases

total_matrisome_proteins_identified.interpretation: Comprehensive identification of kidney matrisome components.

novel_proteins.interpretation: Proteins not previously listed in the Matrisome Database, expanding the reference.

quantified_proteins.interpretation: Distinct matrisome proteins successfully quantified using MaxQuant LFQ.

core_matrisome_percentage_cme.interpretation: Proportion of core matrisome proteins detected by CME method.

core_matrisome_percentage_sme.interpretation: Proportion of core matrisome proteins detected by SME method.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. The study successfully characterized the mouse kidney matrisome composition, identified 22 novel proteins, created a reference database, and demonstrated the utility of combining CME and SME extraction methods for comprehensive ECM profiling.
